summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment variables to attacker-controlled Azure Blob endpoint
details: electron-orbit@1.0.33 performs a two-stage credential-theft operation. At npm install time, package.json's postinstall hook runs install.js, which serializes the installer's process.env (all keys not containing 'path', case-insensitive) as JSON and writes it to /tmp/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. Access to process.env is obfuscated via an XOR+index string decoder (__s), reaching globalThis['process']['env'] indirectly to defeat static scanners. On subsequent require()/import of the package, top-level code in dist/index.js reads the marker file, XOR-encodes its contents with the key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes the result, and embeds it into a URL query string sent to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net (path /icons/orbit.png?...). The request is transmitted via a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (src/aetherls.ts implements the full ClientHello/handshake/AEAD sequence over raw net.connect(443, host)), bypassing Node's https module and common network instrumentation. Activation of the live exfil URL is gated on a SHA-256 match against process.env.BuildType (hardcoded digest 0ceaa396e2134a9937563e841539f30a9995e6e8646e60dcec24b26dc5748295), an environmental trigger typical of targeted anti-analysis payloads. The package name and declared author ('OrbitDB Archive', homepage github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) impersonate the archived OrbitDB project to increase install rate. Installer harm: CI/CD tokens, cloud credentials, and other secrets held in environment variables are transmitted off-host to an attacker-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
